title: BUYING A SERVICE PLAN
conversation: Tom: Hi, I'm in the market for a new cell phone plan. Mike: Do you have a plan now? Tom: Yes, with MobileOne. But it's about to expire. Mike: You're not happy with them? Tom: No, I'm not. Their service is terrible. My calls are always breaking up. Mike: Cellular Star's service is first rate. You'll get great reception. What are you looking for in a plan? Tom: I need 400 minutes a month for daytime calls. I'd like unlimited night and weekend calling. Mike: What about call forwarding, voice mail, and text messaging? Tom: I don't need any of those bells and whistles. Mike: The Choice 450 is our no-frills plan. That'll run you $39.99 a month, plus tax. Tom: That doesn't include long-distance calls, does it? Mike: Yes, it does. Tom: So it's $39.99 a month, plus tax. Mike: Yes, and there's a one-time fee of $35. That's for setting up the account. Tom: Any hidden fees? Mike: No. Of course, you'll want to read the fine print of your contract. Tom: Right. I don't want to get stuck with a plan that only lets me make long-distance calls between midnight and 3 a.m. Mike: Did I mention that if you sign up for this plan by Friday, we'll throw in a free phone? Tom: I could use a new phone. Mike: It's a great offer, with no strings attached. All set to sign up? Tom: Before I sign on the dotted line, I'd better make sure I know what I'm getting into.

idioms.meaning_en: Interested in purchasing or acquiring something
idioms.meaning_vi: có nhu cầu mua cái gì đó
idioms.example: I'm in the market for a new laptop since my old one broke.
idioms.context: Hi, I'm in the market for a new cell phone plan.

idioms.meaning_en: When a telephone signal is being interrupted so that the caller's voice is not clear
idioms.meaning_vi: tín hiệu bị chập chờn, nghe không rõ
idioms.example: Can you hear me? You're breaking up because the signal is weak.
idioms.context: My calls are always breaking up.

idioms.meaning_vi: chất lượng hàng đầu, tuyệt vời
idioms.example: The service at this hotel is absolutely first rate.
idioms.context: Cellular Star's service is first rate.

idioms.meaning_en: Extra features added to a product to make it more attractive, though not essential
idioms.meaning_vi: những tính năng phụ trợ hào nhoáng (không quá cần thiết)
idioms.example: I just want a simple phone without all the expensive bells and whistles.
idioms.context: I don't need any of those bells and whistles.

idioms.meaning_en: Providing only the basic essentials; without extra features
idioms.meaning_vi: dịch vụ cơ bản, không có tiện ích dư thừa
idioms.example: We stayed at a no-frills hotel to save money on our trip.
idioms.context: The Choice 450 is our no-frills plan.

idioms.meaning_en: To cost someone a specific amount of money
idioms.meaning_vi: tiêu tốn của bạn (bao nhiêu tiền)
idioms.example: A new engine for that car will run you about two thousand dollars.
idioms.context: That'll run you $39.99 a month, plus tax.

idioms.meaning_en: A charge that is paid only once
idioms.meaning_vi: phí đóng một lần duy nhất
idioms.example: There is a small one-time fee to join the club.
idioms.context: Yes, and there's a one-time fee of $35.

idioms.meaning_en: The process of establishing or starting something
idioms.example: He spent the whole afternoon setting up his new computer.
idioms.context: That's for setting up the account.

idioms.meaning_en: Expenses that are not clearly stated or apparent at the start
idioms.meaning_vi: các loại phí ẩn, chi phí ngầm
idioms.example: Make sure to check the contract for any hidden fees before signing.

idioms.meaning_en: Important details of a legal document that are written in small heavy-to-read text
idioms.meaning_vi: điều khoản in chữ nhỏ (thường chứa thông tin quan trọng/bất lợi)
idioms.example: Always read the fine print before you sign a loan agreement.
idioms.context: Of course, you'll want to read the fine print of your contract.

idioms.meaning_en: To be forced to deal with someone or something unpleasant
idioms.meaning_vi: bị mắc kẹt với, phải chịu đựng (điều gì đó không mong muốn)
idioms.example: I got stuck with the bill because my friends forgot their wallets.
idioms.context: I don't want to get stuck with a plan that only lets me make long-distance calls between midnight and 3 a.m.

idioms.meaning_en: To include something extra in a deal for free
idioms.meaning_vi: tặng kèm, đưa thêm vào (miễn phí)
idioms.example: If you buy the sofa today, we'll throw in two matching cushions.
idioms.context: Did I mention that if you sign up for this plan by Friday, we'll throw in a free phone?

idioms.meaning_en: To need or benefit from something
idioms.meaning_vi: cần hoặc có thể tận dụng điều gì đó
idioms.example: I'm exhausted; I could use a cup of strong coffee.
idioms.context: I could use a new phone.

idioms.meaning_en: Without any special conditions, restrictions, or hidden requirements
idioms.meaning_vi: không có điều kiện ràng buộc, không kèm theo yêu cầu gì
idioms.example: They offered me a free trial with no strings attached.
idioms.context: It's a great offer, with no strings attached.

idioms.meaning_vi: đã sẵn sàng để làm gì đó
idioms.example: Are you all set to leave for the airport?

idioms.meaning_en: To sign a formal contract or agreement
idioms.meaning_vi: ký tên vào hợp đồng chính thức
idioms.example: Once you sign on the dotted line, the house is officially yours.
idioms.context: Before I sign on the dotted line, I'd better make sure I know what I'm getting into.

idioms.meaning_en: Becoming involved in a situation, often a complex or difficult one
idioms.meaning_vi: tham gia vào, vướng vào (tình huống hoặc thỏa thuận)
idioms.example: He didn't realize how much work he was getting into when he volunteered.
idioms.context: I'd better make sure I know what I'm getting into.
